name: Pagination Playground
summary: The runnable home for the Pagination block — the blocks-layer realization of the Collection Operations `page` stage. Each card pairs a page-state with a contract (pageMode × advance, urlSync, rangeLabel), renders the controls, and runs the same a11y/SEO audit the CI conformance suite asserts. Backlog #036.
description:
Concept

A live surface for the Pagination block, which realizes the page stage of the Collection Operations intent. For each shared fixture it shows the contract options, renders the controls through the reference renderer, shows the produced HTML, and runs the contract audit — a green badge means the rendered controls satisfy the verified accessibility/SEO contract.

Verified contract

The audit checks the WAI-ARIA APG pattern — a <nav aria-label="pagination"> landmark with aria-current="page" — plus the SEO-safe self-canonical ?page=n default, the result-range label's known-total requirement, and infinite scroll rendered as a scroll sentinel (append + auto), never a peer mode.

No drift

The renderer, the audit, and the fixtures are the one shared source the CI conformance suite runs, so the badges here and CI exercise the exact same code. The renderer is a deterministic reference; concrete strategies (a real URL-state provider, windowing) live in Frontier UI.
